competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement - Units and Measurements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, uncertainty analysis, dimensional analysis, and their applications.
competencies: Identify fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities
Apply rules of significant figures in calculations involving measured values
Determine the uncertainty in results based on instrument precision
Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships between physical quantities

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to describing motion using differentiation and integration, analyzing uniform and non-uniform motion, and interpreting kinematic graphs.
competencies: Describe motion of an object moving along a straight line using frame of reference concepts
Apply calculus (differentiation/integration) to derive relations for uniformly accelerated motion
Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs to analyze motion characteristics
Distinguish between average speed, average velocity, and instantaneous velocity

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector operations, resolution of vectors, and analysis of projectile motion and uniform circular motion.
competencies: Perform addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication of vectors using rectangular components
Calculate dot product (scalar) and cross product (vector) of two vectors in a plane
Analyze the trajectory of projectiles under gravity assuming constant acceleration
Derive expressions for velocity and position in uniform circular motion

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion - Newton's Laws and Friction
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding inertia, momentum, impulse, conservation laws, equilibrium conditions, and frictional forces.
competencies: Explain the concept of force and inertia using Newton's First Law
Apply Newton's Second Law (F=ma) and Third Law to solve dynamics problems involving collisions
Use the principle of conservation of linear momentum in isolated systems
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces including static, kinetic, rolling friction, and lubrication

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power - Conservation Laws
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to work-energy theorem, potential energy concepts, conservative forces, and collision analysis.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces using integration or graphical methods
Apply the Work-Energy Theorem to relate net work to change in kinetic energy
Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces based on path independence of work
Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions (1D and 2D) conserving momentum and/or kinetic energy

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion - Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to center of mass motion, torque, angular momentum conservation, rotational kinematics, and moment of inertia.
competencies: Determine the position of the center of mass for systems of particles and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod)
Apply Newton's Second Law for rotation: Torque = I * alpha
Use conservation of angular momentum to analyze rotational interactions
Calculate moment of inertia and radius of gyration for simple geometrical objects
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation - Universal Law and Orbital Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Kepler's laws, gravitational potential energy, escape speed, and satellite motion.
competencies: Apply Newton's Universal Law of Gravitation to calculate forces between celestial bodies
Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth from Earth's surface
Calculate orbital velocity and escape speed for satellites orbiting a planet
Relate gravitational potential energy to work done against gravitational force

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Solids and Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to elasticity, stress-strain relationships, fluid pressure, viscosity, surface tension, and Bernoulli's theorem.
competencies: Apply Hooke's Law and calculate Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus, and Poisson's ratio
Analyze the behavior of fluids under hydrostatic conditions using Pascal's law
Calculate terminal velocity and apply Stokes' law for viscous drag on spherical bodies
Use Bernoulli's theorem to analyze fluid flow in pipes and explain phenomena like dynamic lift

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties - Heat and Thermodynamics Basics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to specific heat, calorimetry, phase changes, modes of heat transfer, and blackbody radiation laws.
competencies: Calculate heat required for temperature change using specific heat capacity (Cp, Cv)
Apply the principle of calorimetry to solve problems involving mixing substances
Analyze latent heat effects during phase transitions (melting/boiling)
Compare conduction, convection, and radiation as modes of heat transfer

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics - Laws and Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermodynamic state variables, laws of thermodynamics, and analysis of gaseous processes.
competencies: Apply the Zeroth Law to define temperature and thermal equilibrium
Use the First Law (conservation of energy) for closed systems involving heat and work
Analyze reversible/irreversible isothermal and adiabatic processes in ideal gases
Evaluate efficiency and performance of cyclic thermodynamic engines

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory - Gas Behavior
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the kinetic theory of gases, molecular speed distributions, and equipartition theorem.
competencies: Derive pressure exerted by an ideal gas using kinetic theory assumptions
Calculate root-mean-square (rms) speed and mean free path of gas molecules
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to determine specific heat capacities of gases
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations - Simple Harmonic Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic motion, SHM equations, energy in oscillation, and simple pendulums.
competencies: Identify conditions for Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM) and derive its differential equation
Analyze the relationship between uniform circular motion and SHM using phase concepts
Calculate time period of a loaded spring and a simple pendulum

competency_groups.title: Waves - Wave Motion and Superposition
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, beats, and interference.
competencies: Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and their propagation speeds

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics - Charges, Fields, and Flux
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Coulomb's law, electric fields due to various charge distributions, Gauss's theorem applications, and dipoles.
competencies: Calculate electrostatic force between point charges using superposition principle

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential - Energy and Capacitance
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to electric potential, equipotential surfaces, dielectrics, capacitor combinations, and energy storage.
competencies: Calculate electric potential due to point charges, dipoles, and systems of charges

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity - Ohm's Law and Circuits
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to drift velocity, resistivity, internal resistance of cells, Kirchhoff's laws, and Wheatstone bridge.
competencies: Apply Ohm's law to analyze linear and non-linear V-I characteristics

competency_groups.title: Magnetism - Fields, Forces, and Torque
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, magnetic forces on charges/conductors, torque on dipoles, and galvanometer conversion.
competencies: Calculate magnetic field due to current-carrying conductors using symmetry arguments
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–4: Moving Charges and Magnetism / Chapter–5: Magnetism and Matter

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction - Faraday's Laws
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to induced EMF, Lenz's law, self/mutual induction, AC circuits (reactance/impedance), and resonance.
competencies: Apply Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction to calculate induced emf
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–6: Electromagnetic Induction / Chapter–7: Alternating Current

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves - Spectrum and Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to displacement current, transverse nature of EM waves, and characteristics of the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Explain the generation of electromagnetic waves through oscillating charges

competency_groups.title: Optics - Ray and Wave Optics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reflection/refraction, lens/mirror formulas, optical instruments, interference, diffraction, and Huygens' principle.
competencies: Apply mirror and lens formulas to determine image properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics / Chapter–10: Wave Optics

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature - Photoelectric Effect and Matter Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to photoelectric effect, Einstein's equation, de-Broglie wavelength, and particle-wave duality.
competencies: Explain the experimental observations of the photoelectric effect using quantum theory
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms - Rutherford and Bohr Models
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to atomic structure, scattering experiments, energy levels in hydrogen atom, and spectral lines.
competencies: Describe the limitations of classical physics leading to quantum models

competency_groups.title: Electronic Devices - Semiconductors and Diodes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to energy bands, p-n junctions, diode characteristics (forward/reverse bias), rectification, and basic semiconductor devices.
competencies: Analyze the I-V characteristics of a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
